action.description: Emergency Orange for CTA backgrounds and decorative accents only — NOT for text on white/light surfaces

contrast_rules.description: WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text (≥18pt or ≥14pt bold). Contrast ratios below are pre-computed.

approved_pairs.usage: Navy text or icons on orange backgrounds — buttons, emergency strip, accents
White text on navy backgrounds — header, footer, booking section
Orange text/icons on navy backgrounds — booking section labels, footer accents
Navy text on white — body copy, headings, card content
Muted gray (gray-500) for secondary text on white — dates, footnotes, metadata
Muted gray (gray-500) on brand-clean — acceptable for secondary text

forbidden_pairs.reason: FAILS WCAG AA — white text on brand-orange is unreadable for low-vision users. Use text-brand-navy instead.
FAILS WCAG AA — brand-orange text on white/light surfaces. Use text-brand-navy for eyebrow labels and inline links on light backgrounds.
FAILS WCAG AA — brand-orange text on brand-clean surface. Use text-brand-navy.
FAILS WCAG AA — gray-400 on white/light. Use gray-500 (#6B7280) or darker for muted text.

landmarks: Every page must have exactly one <main> element wrapping all content between <header> and <footer>.
The <header> element provides the 'banner' landmark. Do not add role='banner' explicitly.
The <footer> element provides the 'contentinfo' landmark. Do not add role='contentinfo' explicitly.
Use aria-labelledby on <section> elements that have a visible heading (h2) inside them.
Use aria-label on <section> elements that have no visible heading (e.g. hero, trust bar).
aria_attribute_restrictions: aria-label is only valid on elements that have an implicit or explicit ARIA role. Never add aria-label to a plain <div> or <span> with no role.
To label a decorative group of icons (e.g. star ratings), add role='img' aria-label='...' to the container.
If the rating/value is already expressed in adjacent visible text (e.g. '4.9 Google Rating'), prefer aria-hidden='true' on the icon group instead.
Do not add aria-label to interactive elements whose text label already fully describes the action.
